options.text_en: Combine multiple models using ensemble learning
options.explanation_en: Ensemble learning combines multiple independent models to improve performance, but it is less efficient than reusing the characteristics of pre-trained models.

options.text_en: Discover hidden patterns using unsupervised learning
options.explanation_en: Unsupervised learning is a technique for finding patterns in unlabeled data, but this scenario requires a supervised learning task such as product classification.

options.text_en: Reuse pre-trained models through transfer learning
options.explanation_en: Transfer learning involves reusing low-level visual features learned from general object detection as initial values for a product classification model. This achieves fast learning convergence and high accuracy even with limited datasets.

options.text_en: Optimize reward signals using reinforcement learning
options.explanation_en: Reinforcement learning is a technique where an agent interacts with the environment to maximize rewards, but it is not suitable for supervised learning tasks such as product image classification.

options.text_en: Access a pre-trained model hub and quickly fine-tune selected models with your own data
options.explanation_en: SageMaker JumpStart provides immediate access to hundreds of pre-trained models that can be rapidly customized to fit your business needs.

options.text_en: Deployment is available only on on-premises infrastructure and does not integrate with AWS Cloud
options.explanation_en: JumpStart is a fully cloud-based service that is integrated with AWS Cloud infrastructure and is not exclusively on-premises.

options.text_en: Automatically deploy all models to production and completely skip quality validation steps
options.explanation_en: While JumpStart supports model selection and fine-tuning, model evaluation and validation are mandatory before production deployment.

options.text_en: Provide only algorithm libraries and require complex coding for model execution
options.explanation_en: JumpStart offers a user-friendly interface and pre-configured models that enable model development with minimal coding.

options.text_en: Amazon Relational Database Service (RDS)
options.explanation_en: RDS is a SQL-based relational database that is not specialized for vector embedding storage and semantic search.

options.text_en: Amazon DynamoDB NoSQL database
options.explanation_en: DynamoDB is a key-value based NoSQL store that is not specialized for large-scale vector search.

options.text_en: Amazon OpenSearch Serverless
options.explanation_en: OpenSearch Serverless is the default vector database for Bedrock Knowledge Bases, providing vector embedding storage and semantic search capabilities by default.

options.text_en: Amazon Redshift data warehouse
options.explanation_en: Redshift is an analytics-focused data warehouse that is not suitable for real-time semantic search use cases.

options.text_en: Agents for Amazon Bedrock
options.explanation_en: Agents for Amazon Bedrock enable LLMs to autonomously call external tools and APIs to plan and execute multi-step tasks. It is optimized for complex automation workflows like AWS cost optimization.

options.text_en: Retrain models from scratch using large datasets
options.explanation_en: Model fine-tuning is useful for custom domain learning, but it does not enable real-time API calls and automated task execution.

options.text_en: Chatbot interface that returns only a single response to user questions
options.explanation_en: Standard chatbots support only simple Q&A and do not support multi-step task planning and automated execution.

options.text_en: Safety guardrails that filter harmful content from model responses
options.explanation_en: Guardrails are filtering functions that ensure response safety, but do not provide external API integration and automated task execution capabilities.

options.text_en: Availability Zones are logical groups of data centers distributed across multiple regions
options.explanation_en: Availability Zones exist only within a single region and are not distributed across multiple regions.

options.text_en: A region is a single data center, and high availability is provided only through cross-region replication
options.explanation_en: A region is composed of multiple Availability Zones, and 99.99% high availability can already be achieved through multi-AZ deployment.

options.text_en: Each region is composed of at least 2 or more geographically separated Availability Zones, and AZs are physically independent
options.explanation_en: This is a core AWS infrastructure design principle. Each Availability Zone is a physically separate data center with independent power, networking, and cooling systems, which forms the foundation for 99.99% high availability.

options.text_en: Availability Zones are single data centers sharing the same power, networking, and cooling
options.explanation_en: Availability Zones have independent power, networking, and cooling. Shared infrastructure would cause cascading failures and does not align with high availability design principles.
